title: BORON
description: Boron is a trace mineral and semiconductor element that plays a vital role in human metabolism, particularly in the regulation of steroid hormones and bone mineralization. It works by reducing Sex Hormone-Binding Globulin (SHBG) levels to increase free testosterone and improving the half-life of vitamin D and estrogen, while also acting as an anti-inflammatory agent through the modulation of cytokines.

research.title: Pivotal role of boron supplementation on bone health: A narrative review
research.summary: This review of 11 studies confirms that 3 mg/day of boron significantly supports bone mineral density and maintenance by modulating calcium, vitamin D, and sex steroid hormone metabolism.

research.title: A Comparison of Boron Supplement and Tamsulosin as Medical Expulsive Therapy for Urinary Stones After Extracorporeal Shock Wave Lithotripsy: a Randomized Controlled Clinical Trial
research.summary: Boron was found to be an effective medical expulsive therapy for urinary stones by reducing ureteral wall edema and facilitating stone passage.

research.title: The vital roles of boron in animal health and production: A comprehensive review
research.summary: Research indicates boron has pleiotropic effects, including activating immune responses, providing antioxidant detoxification, and preventing osteoporosis.

research.title: Calcium fructoborate--potential anti-inflammatory agent
research.summary: This study highlights boron's role as a superoxide ion scavenger and anti-inflammatory agent that inhibits the release of inflammatory mediators like IL-1β and IL-6.

research.title: Boron intake and prostate cancer risk
research.summary: While some experimental studies suggest a protective effect against prostate cancer, this large cohort study found no significant association between dietary boron intake and cancer risk.

sentiment.summary: Most users report high satisfaction with boron for libido and energy, frequently describing it as a 'game-changer' for hormonal health, though a minority experiences sleep disturbances.

effects.label: Hormonal Balancing:
effects.description: High effectiveness for those with high SHBG; it makes existing testosterone more bioavailable rather than just increasing total production.

effects.label: Bone Density:
effects.description: Research consistently supports its role in preventing bone mineral density loss and improving calcium metabolism.

effects.label: Quick Onset:
effects.description: Users often report noticing physical changes in energy and libido within 3-7 days, which is faster than most other mineral supplements.

effects.label: Bioavailability:
effects.description: It is highly absorbable (85-90%) and acts as a synergist for magnesium and vitamin D absorption.

dosage.label: Standard Therapeutic Dose:
dosage.description: 3 mg daily is the most clinically supported dose for general bone health.

dosage.label: Hormonal Optimization Dose:
dosage.description: Users and some performance studies frequently suggest 6 mg to 12 mg daily for testosterone support.

dosage.label: Cycling Protocol:
dosage.description: Many users recommend a '2 weeks on, 1 week off' or 'weekday only' cycle to prevent perceived dampening of effects or shifts in estrogen.

dosage.label: Upper Limit:
dosage.description: Health organizations suggest an Upper Level (UL) of 10-20 mg daily to avoid potential toxicity.

sideEffects.label: Insomnia:
sideEffects.description: A notable number of users report 'wide-awake' episodes or interrupted sleep, potentially due to increased metabolic or hormonal activity.

sideEffects.label: Irritability:
sideEffects.description: Some users report feeling 'short-tempered' or aggressive as a result of increased free androgen levels.

sideEffects.label: Riboflavin Depletion:
sideEffects.description: Chronic high-dose use may increase urinary excretion of Vitamin B2 (Riboflavin), leading some to suggest taking it with a B-complex.

sideEffects.label: Hormonal Fluctuations:
sideEffects.description: If not cycled, some users worry about a rebound effect or changes in estrogen (E2) levels over time.

availability.label: Cost-Effectiveness:
availability.description: Boron is widely recognized as one of the cheapest supplements available, costing mere cents per dose.

availability.label: Form Variations:
availability.description: Available commonly as Boron Citrate, Glycinate, or Calcium Fructoborate (a natural plant-form ester).
